articles.headline: C. P. Radhakrishnan Sworn In as India's 15th Vice President
articles.explainer:
• C. P. Radhakrishnan took the oath of office as the 15th Vice President of India, administered by the President of India at Rashtrapati Bhavan.
• His election followed a comprehensive electoral process involving members of both Houses of Parliament.
• The Vice President serves as the ex-officio Chairman of the Rajya Sabha, presiding over its sessions and maintaining decorum.
• The office plays a crucial role in the constitutional setup, acting as a link between the Executive and the Legislature.
• He previously served as Governor and was a prominent political figure, bringing significant administrative and legislative experience to the role.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies.
prelimsPoints: The Vice President is the second-highest constitutional office in India.
Elected by an electoral college consisting of members of both Houses of Parliament (Article 66).
Administered oath by the President (Article 69).
Ex-officio Chairman of the Rajya Sabha (Article 64).
Acts as President in case of vacancy or absence of the President (Article 65).
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the Vice President's role in India's parliamentary democracy, particularly as the Chairman of the Rajya Sabha.
Analyze the constitutional powers and functions of the Vice President and their impact on legislative proceedings.
Evaluate the electoral process for the Vice President and its democratic implications.
Examine the checks and balances provided by the office of the Vice President in the Indian constitutional framework.
articles.imageDescription: C. P. Radhakrishnan taking oath as the 15th Vice President of India, with the President of India present.
articles.trivia: India's first Vice President, Dr. Sarvepalli Radhakrishnan, later became the second President of India.

articles.headline: Ten Naxalites Killed in Major Clash with Security Forces in Chhattisgarh's Gariaband
articles.explainer:
• A significant anti-Naxal operation in Chhattisgarh's Gariaband district resulted in the killing of ten Naxalites during a fierce encounter with security forces.
• The operation was a joint effort by district reserve guard (DRG) and special task force (STF) personnel, targeting a known Naxal hideout.
• This incident underscores the ongoing challenge of Left-Wing Extremism (LWE) in several parts of India, particularly in the 'Red Corridor' states.
• Security forces recovered a cache of arms, ammunition, and other Naxal literature from the encounter site.
• The government continues its dual strategy of robust security operations combined with development initiatives to combat LWE.
gsSubTopics: Linkages between development and spread of extremism.
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security.

Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention.
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
prelimsPoints: Gariaband district is located in Chhattisgarh, a state significantly affected by LWE.
Security forces involved in anti-Naxal operations include CRPF, BSF, ITBP, and state police forces like DRG and STF.
The 'Red Corridor' refers to regions in central, eastern, and southern India affected by Naxalite-Maoist insurgency.

Key strategies against LWE include 'SAMADHAN' (Smart leadership, Aggressive strategy, Motivation and training, Actionable intelligence, Dashboard-based KPIs, Harnessing technology, Action plan for each theatre, No access to financing).
mainsPoints: Analyze the root causes and socio-economic factors contributing to the spread of Left-Wing Extremism in India.
Discuss the effectiveness of India's multi-pronged strategy (security and development) in tackling LWE, citing recent successes and challenges.
Examine the human rights concerns and ethical dilemmas faced by security forces in counter-insurgency operations.
Suggest policy measures for sustainable peace and development in Naxal-affected regions, focusing on infrastructure, governance, and tribal rights.
articles.imageDescription: Security forces patrolling a forested area in Chhattisgarh during an anti-Naxal operation.
articles.trivia: The term 'Naxalite' originated from the Naxalbari village in West Bengal, where a peasant uprising took place in 1967.

articles.headline: Tragic Truck Accident Claims 10 Lives During Ganesh Chaturthi Celebrations in Karnataka's Hassan District
articles.explainer:
• Ten people were killed and 22 others injured when a speeding truck ran over a crowd celebrating Ganesh Chaturthi in Hassan district, Karnataka.
• The incident highlights critical issues related to road safety, crowd management during festivals, and enforcement of traffic regulations.
• Local authorities have launched an investigation into the cause of the accident, including potential negligence by the driver and event organizers.
• The state government has announced ex-gratia payments for the victims and assured support for the injured.
• This tragedy underscores the need for stricter adherence to safety protocols and better infrastructure management, especially during large public gatherings.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of Indian Society, Diversity of India.
Social empowerment, communalism, regionalism & secularism.
Urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
prelimsPoints: Ganesh Chaturthi is a popular Hindu festival celebrating the birth of Lord Ganesha.
The Motor Vehicles (Amendment) Act, 2019, aims to improve road safety by imposing stricter penalties.
National Road Safety Policy focuses on education, engineering (roads and vehicles), enforcement, and emergency care.
Disaster Management Act, 2005, provides for effective management of disasters and matters connected therewith.
mainsPoints: Analyze the multi-faceted challenges in ensuring road safety in India, including driver behavior, infrastructure deficiencies, and enforcement gaps.
Discuss effective strategies for crowd management during religious festivals and public events to prevent such tragedies.
Evaluate the role of technology and public awareness campaigns in promoting safer road practices and reducing accident fatalities.
Examine the institutional framework for disaster response and victim compensation in India, suggesting areas for improvement.
articles.imageDescription: Emergency services at the site of a truck accident in Hassan, Karnataka, with local residents gathered.
articles.trivia: India accounts for about 11% of the total number of road accident deaths in the world, despite having only 1% of the world's vehicles.

articles.headline: Former Kerala Assembly Speaker P. P. Thankachan Passes Away at 86
articles.explainer:
• P. P. Thankachan, a veteran politician and former Speaker of the Kerala Legislative Assembly, passed away at the age of 86.
• He served as a member of the Kerala Legislative Assembly from 1982 to 2001 and held the Speaker's position from 1991 to 1995.
• Thankachan was known for his significant contributions to state politics and his role in upholding parliamentary traditions.
• His career spanned several decades, witnessing crucial legislative developments and political shifts in Kerala.
• The demise marks the end of an era for a prominent voice in Kerala's political landscape.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary Ministries and Departments of the Government; pressure groups and formal/informal associations and their role in the Polity.
prelimsPoints: The Speaker of a State Legislative Assembly is the presiding officer of the Assembly.
Elected by the members of the Assembly from amongst themselves.
Maintains order and decorum, interprets rules, and certifies money bills.
Can be removed by a resolution passed by a majority of all the then members of the Assembly.
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical role of the Speaker in ensuring the smooth functioning and integrity of a State Legislative Assembly.
Analyze how the Speaker's position contributes to maintaining parliamentary democracy at the state level.
Examine the challenges faced by Speakers in upholding impartiality amidst political pressures.
articles.imageDescription: A portrait of P. P. Thankachan, former Speaker of the Kerala Legislative Assembly.
articles.trivia: The first woman Speaker of any State Legislative Assembly in India was Shanno Devi (Haryana, 1966).

articles.headline: India Reviews Progress on India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor (IMEC) with Key Partners
articles.explainer:
• India held a high-level review meeting with partner nations to assess the progress and future roadmap of the India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor (IMEC).
• IMEC, announced during the G20 Summit in 2023, aims to enhance connectivity and economic integration across Asia, the Middle East, and Europe through sea and rail links.
• Discussions focused on accelerating infrastructure development, streamlining customs procedures, and ensuring financial viability of the ambitious project.
• The corridor is envisioned as a counter-narrative to other major connectivity initiatives, promoting stable and resilient supply chains.
• This initiative holds significant geopolitical and geoeconomic importance for India, enhancing its strategic outreach and trade routes.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: IMEC was launched during the G20 Summit in New Delhi in September 2023.
It comprises an Eastern Corridor (India to Arabian Gulf) and a Northern Corridor (Arabian Gulf to Europe).
Key partners include India, USA, Saudi Arabia, UAE, France, Germany, Italy, and the European Union.
Aims to include a railway network, ship-to-rail transit networks, and road transport routes.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic and economic significance of the IMEC for India in the context of global connectivity and trade.
Compare and contrast IMEC with other major connectivity initiatives, discussing its potential advantages and challenges.
Discuss how IMEC can foster greater regional cooperation and stability in the Middle East and Europe.
Examine the potential geopolitical implications of IMEC on India's foreign policy and its role in the Indo-Pacific.
articles.imageDescription: A map illustrating the proposed routes of the India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor.
articles.trivia: The IMEC project is seen as a major outcome of India's G20 presidency in 2023.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Earth Observation Satellite for Enhanced Resource Mapping
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched an advanced Earth Observation Satellite (EOS) from Sriharikota, marking another milestone in India's space program.
• The satellite, carried by a PSLV rocket, is equipped with high-resolution imaging sensors for detailed mapping of natural resources, agriculture, and urban development.
• Its data will be crucial for disaster management, environmental monitoring, and climate change studies, providing timely and accurate information.
• This mission reinforces India's indigenous capabilities in satellite technology and its commitment to using space for national development.
• The launch further strengthens India's constellation of remote sensing satellites, offering comprehensive coverage and data services.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: ISRO is India's national space agency.
PSLV (Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle) is ISRO's third-generation launch vehicle, primarily used for launching Earth observation and remote sensing satellites.
Earth observation satellites provide data for applications like agriculture, forestry, urban planning, disaster warning, and oceanography.
Sriharikota in Andhra Pradesh is India's primary spaceport, home to the Satish Dhawan Space Centre (SDSC).
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of Earth Observation Satellites in addressing India's developmental challenges, including food security, water management, and disaster mitigation.
Analyze India's achievements in space technology and its contributions to global space exploration and applications.
Examine the socio-economic benefits derived from space-based applications in various sectors of the Indian economy.
Discuss the challenges and future prospects of India's space program, including the increasing role of private sector participation.
articles.imageDescription: A PSLV rocket lifting off from the launch pad at Sriharikota, carrying an Earth Observation Satellite.
articles.trivia: India is one of the few countries with advanced capabilities in building and launching remote sensing satellites, with its first such satellite, Bhaskara-I, launched in 1979.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Reinforces Tribal Rights, Upholds Amendments to Forest Rights Act
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark judgment, upheld recent amendments to the Forest Rights Act (FRA), further strengthening the rights of tribal communities and forest dwellers.
• The ruling clarifies the process for recognizing community forest resource (CFR) rights and individual forest rights (IFRs), emphasizing Gram Sabha's pivotal role.
• Environmental groups and tribal organizations had challenged certain aspects, but the court affirmed the amendments' intent to balance conservation with tribal welfare.
• This decision is expected to have significant implications for forest governance, conservation efforts, and the empowerment of indigenous communities across the country.
• The judgment reiterated the 'prior informed consent' principle for any developmental projects in tribal areas.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: The Scheduled Tribes and Other Traditional Forest Dwellers (Recognition of Forest Rights) Act, 2006 (FRA), aims to recognize and vest forest rights and occupation in forest land to forest dwelling Scheduled Tribes and Other Traditional Forest Dwellers.
Gram Sabha plays a crucial role in the process of recognition and vesting of forest rights.
The Act recognizes four types of rights: Title rights, Use rights, Relief and development rights, and Forest management rights.
Community Forest Resource (CFR) rights empower Gram Sabhas to protect, regenerate, conserve, or manage any forest resources.
mainsPoints: Analyze the impact of the Forest Rights Act, 2006, on tribal empowerment and forest conservation in India, critically evaluating its implementation.
Discuss the role of the judiciary in balancing environmental protection with the rights of indigenous communities, with reference to recent judgments.
Examine the challenges in securing land and resource rights for tribal populations and suggest policy reforms for their effective implementation.
Evaluate the concept of 'prior informed consent' in the context of developmental projects in tribal areas and its implications for sustainable development.
articles.imageDescription: A group of tribal community members gathered in a forest, symbolizing their connection to forest resources.
articles.trivia: India has over 104 million tribal people, accounting for 8.6% of the country's population, as per the 2011 Census.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate, Focuses on Inflation Control Amid Global Uncertainties
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its latest meeting, deciding to maintain the repo rate at its current level.
• The decision reflects a cautious approach, balancing inflation control with the need to support economic growth amidst global economic uncertainties and domestic inflationary pressures.
• RBI Governor highlighted the global headwinds, including geopolitical tensions and commodity price volatility, as key factors influencing the policy stance.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to aligning inflation with the target while supporting growth, indicating a 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance.
• Experts anticipate continued vigilance from the central bank, with future policy moves dependent on evolving economic data and inflation trajectory.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.

Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.

Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
prelimsPoints: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a six-member body responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) in India.
The primary objective of monetary policy is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
Repo rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks, impacting the cost of borrowing for individuals and businesses.
Inflation targeting framework in India aims to keep Consumer Price Index (CPI) inflation at 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
mainsPoints: Analyze the current challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control and economic growth, considering domestic and global factors.
Discuss the effectiveness of the Monetary Policy Committee's decisions in achieving its mandated objectives of price stability and growth.
Examine the implications of a 'status quo' policy stance on various sectors of the Indian economy, including lending, investment, and consumption.
Evaluate the role of fiscal policy in complementing monetary policy to achieve macroeconomic stability and inclusive growth.
articles.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India headquarters building in Mumbai, symbolizing the central bank's authority.
articles.trivia: The RBI was established in 1935 under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934, and was nationalized in 1949.

articles.headline: Government Reviews Progress of National Quantum Mission, Emphasizes Research and Industry Collaboration
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Science & Technology conducted a high-level review of the National Quantum Mission (NQM), assessing its progress in quantum computing, communication, and sensing.
• The review highlighted significant advancements in indigenous quantum technologies, with several research institutions showcasing prototypes and experimental setups.
• Emphasis was placed on fostering stronger collaboration between academia, industry, and startups to translate research into deployable products and services.
• NQM aims to position India as a leading nation in quantum technologies, addressing challenges in secure communication, advanced computing, and precision sensing.
• Funding allocations and strategic partnerships for the next phase of the mission were also discussed, focusing on skill development and infrastructure.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: National Quantum Mission (NQM) was approved by the Union Cabinet in April 2023 with a total outlay of Rs 6,003.65 crore.
It aims to nurture and scale up scientific and industrial R&D and create a robust ecosystem for quantum technology development.
Quantum computing uses principles of quantum mechanics (superposition, entanglement) to solve complex problems faster than classical computers.
Quantum communication offers ultra-secure communication through quantum key distribution (QKD).
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential transformative impact of quantum technologies on various sectors such as defence, finance, healthcare, and cybersecurity.
Analyze the strategic rationale behind India's National Quantum Mission and its implications for national security and economic competitiveness.
Examine the challenges in developing and deploying quantum technologies, including technological hurdles, talent acquisition, and ethical considerations.
Suggest policy measures to accelerate quantum research and development in India, focusing on public-private partnerships and international collaborations.
articles.imageDescription: Abstract visual representation of quantum computing principles, with glowing nodes and connections.
articles.trivia: The smallest unit of quantum information is a qubit, which unlike classical bits, can exist in a superposition of 0 and 1 simultaneously.

articles.headline: India and France Hold Strategic Dialogue, Deepen Defence and Space Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• India and France held their annual Strategic Dialogue, focusing on enhancing cooperation in defence, space, and emerging technologies.
• The dialogue reaffirmed the strong strategic partnership between the two nations, particularly in the Indo-Pacific region and counter-terrorism efforts.
• Discussions included joint defence manufacturing, technology transfer, and intelligence sharing, building on existing defence procurements.
• Both sides emphasized the importance of a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, aligning their visions for regional stability.
• The leaders also explored new avenues for collaboration in areas like artificial intelligence, cybersecurity, and green energy, reinforcing their comprehensive strategic partnership.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India and France established a strategic partnership in 1998.
France is a key partner for India in defence, space, and civil nuclear energy.
Joint military exercises include Varuna (Navy), Garuda (Air Force), and Shakti (Army).
Both countries are committed to multilateralism and a rules-based international order.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolution and significance of the India-France strategic partnership in the changing geopolitical landscape.
Discuss the key pillars of cooperation between India and France, particularly in defence, space, and clean energy.
Examine how the India-France partnership contributes to stability and security in the Indo-Pacific region.
Evaluate the potential for further collaboration in emerging technologies and its implications for India's technological self-reliance.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and France displayed together, symbolizing bilateral cooperation.
articles.trivia: France was one of the first countries to establish a strategic dialogue with India, reflecting the depth of their relationship.

articles.headline: Government Launches AgriStack Phase II: Digital Public Infrastructure for Agriculture Sector
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare launched the second phase of AgriStack, a digital public infrastructure aimed at modernizing India's agriculture sector.
• AgriStack seeks to create a unified digital ecosystem leveraging technologies like AI, machine learning, and blockchain for data-driven farming.
• Phase II focuses on integrating farmer databases with land records, crop advisory services, and access to credit and insurance, ensuring greater transparency and efficiency.
• The initiative aims to empower farmers with personalized insights, improve supply chain management, and enhance agricultural productivity.
• Concerns regarding data privacy and security for millions of farmers were addressed, with robust protocols put in place.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.

Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: AgriStack is a collection of technologies and digital databases proposed by the government to farmers and the agricultural sector.
It aims to create a 'unified platform for farmers' to provide them with end-to-end services.
Key components include a federated farmers' database, a unified farmer service interface, and a land record digitization project.
The initiative leverages Aadhaar for unique farmer identification and aims to integrate various government schemes like PM-KISAN.
mainsPoints: Analyze the potential benefits and challenges of digitizing India's agriculture sector through initiatives like AgriStack, focusing on farmer empowerment and productivity.
Discuss the role of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) in transforming various sectors of the economy, citing examples from agriculture and other domains.
Examine the ethical considerations and data privacy concerns associated with collecting and utilizing vast amounts of farmer data, and suggest mitigation strategies.
Evaluate how AgriStack can contribute to achieving the government's goal of doubling farmers' income and ensuring food security.
articles.imageDescription: A farmer using a tablet in a field, with digital data overlays, representing the AgriStack initiative.
articles.trivia: India's agriculture sector accounts for about 18% of the country's GDP and employs over 50% of the workforce.

articles.headline: NITI Aayog Report Highlights Best Practices in Climate-Resilient Agriculture for Food Security
articles.explainer:
• NITI Aayog released a comprehensive report detailing best practices and policy recommendations for promoting climate-resilient agriculture across India.
• The report identifies successful indigenous and scientific farming methods that help farmers adapt to changing climate patterns, including extreme weather events.
• Key recommendations include promoting drought-resistant crop varieties, efficient water management techniques, and climate-smart agricultural practices.
• The initiative aims to enhance food security, protect farmer livelihoods, and contribute to India's climate action goals.
• The report stresses the need for greater investment in agricultural research, extension services, and financial support for farmers adopting these practices.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.

Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
keyTerms: National Adaptation Fund for Climate Change
prelimsPoints: NITI Aayog is the premier policy 'Think Tank' of the Government of India, providing directional and policy inputs.
Climate-resilient agriculture involves practices that help agricultural systems adapt to climate change and mitigate greenhouse gas emissions.
Examples include conservation agriculture, precision farming, crop diversification, and improved irrigation efficiency.
National Adaptation Fund for Climate Change (NAFCC) was established to support concrete adaptation activities vulnerable to adverse effects of climate change.
mainsPoints: Analyze the multi-dimensional challenges posed by climate change to Indian agriculture and its implications for food security and rural livelihoods.
Discuss the various strategies and best practices for developing climate-resilient agriculture in India, citing examples from the NITI Aayog report.
Examine the role of government policies, technological innovations, and community participation in promoting sustainable and climate-smart farming practices.
Evaluate the economic and social benefits of transitioning to climate-resilient agriculture, including enhanced farmer income and environmental sustainability.
articles.imageDescription: Farmers working in a field with advanced irrigation systems, symbolizing climate-resilient agriculture.
articles.trivia: India is one of the top 10 countries most affected by extreme weather events, making climate resilience in agriculture crucial.

articles.headline: India Nominates Traditional Art Form for UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage List
articles.explainer:
• India has formally nominated a unique traditional art form from one of its states for inscription on UNESCO's Representative List of the Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity.
• The nomination highlights the rich cultural diversity and historical significance of the art, which encompasses traditional performing arts, rituals, and craftsmanship.
• Extensive documentation and community participation were involved in preparing the nomination dossier, emphasizing the living traditions and their intergenerational transmission.
• If inscribed, it would bring global recognition to the art form, aiding in its preservation and promoting cultural tourism.
• This move underscores India's commitment to safeguarding its diverse cultural heritage and sharing it with the world.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
prelimsPoints: UNESCO's Intangible Cultural Heritage list aims to safeguard important intangible cultural heritage worldwide.
India has several elements inscribed on this list, including Yoga, Kumbh Mela, and Traditional brass and copper craft of utensil making among the Thatheras of Jandiala Guru.
The Ministry of Culture is the nodal agency for coordinating India's nominations to UNESCO's ICH list.
ICH includes oral traditions, performing arts, social practices, rituals, festive events, knowledge and practices concerning nature and the universe, and traditional craftsmanship.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of UNESCO's Intangible Cultural Heritage list in preserving and promoting India's diverse living traditions.
Analyze the challenges involved in safeguarding traditional art forms and cultural practices in the face of modernization and globalization.
Examine the socio-economic benefits of inscribing an art form on the UNESCO list, including its impact on local communities and cultural tourism.
Suggest strategies for effective documentation, transmission, and promotion of India's intangible cultural heritage.
articles.imageDescription: Artists performing a vibrant traditional Indian art form, showcasing intricate costumes and expressions.
articles.trivia: The Convention for the Safeguarding of the Intangible Cultural Heritage was adopted by UNESCO in 2003.

articles.headline: India Advocates for Global South at UN General Assembly, Calls for Multilateral Reforms
articles.explainer:
• India's External Affairs Minister addressed the UN General Assembly (UNGA), emphasizing the need for greater representation and voice for the Global South in global governance.
• The speech highlighted India's commitment to multilateralism but stressed the urgency of reforming international institutions, including the UN Security Council.
• Key issues raised included climate justice, equitable access to resources, sustainable development goals (SDGs), and addressing global inequalities.
• India reiterated its stance on counter-terrorism and called for a comprehensive convention to tackle the menace effectively.
• The address underscored India's growing leadership role as a voice for developing nations and its vision for a more inclusive world order.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: The UN General Assembly (UNGA) is the main deliberative, policymaking, and representative organ of the United Nations.
The concept of 'Global South' refers to countries in Asia, Africa, and Latin America, often characterized by developing economies and shared historical experiences.
India has consistently advocated for reforms of the UN Security Council, seeking permanent membership.
Climate Justice emphasizes that those who have contributed least to climate change suffer the most from its impacts and should receive support.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's increasing role as a voice for the Global South and its implications for global governance and international relations.
Discuss the necessity and challenges of reforming multilateral institutions like the United Nations, particularly the Security Council, to reflect contemporary realities.
Examine India's stance on key global issues such as climate change, sustainable development, and counter-terrorism at international forums.
Evaluate the effectiveness of India's diplomatic efforts in advocating for the interests of developing nations on the world stage.
articles.imageDescription: The United Nations General Assembly hall during a session, with country flags visible.
articles.trivia: The first session of the UN General Assembly was convened on 10 January 1946 in London.

articles.headline: Defence Acquisition Council Approves Multi-Crore Indigenisation Projects for Armed Forces
articles.explainer:
• The Defence Acquisition Council (DAC), chaired by the Defence Minister, approved several capital acquisition proposals worth multi-crore rupees, focusing on indigenous procurement.
• The approvals aim to significantly boost the 'Make in India' and 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat' initiatives in the defence sector, reducing reliance on foreign imports.
• Projects include new generation missile systems, advanced surveillance equipment, and upgrades for existing platforms for the Army, Navy, and Air Force.
• The DAC emphasized faster decision-making and streamlined procurement processes to ensure timely delivery of critical equipment to the armed forces.
• This move is expected to invigorate the domestic defence manufacturing ecosystem, fostering innovation and creating jobs.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology.

Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention.
prelimsPoints: The Defence Acquisition Council (DAC) is the highest decision-making body in the Ministry of Defence for defence procurements.
It is chaired by the Defence Minister.
The 'Make in India' initiative aims to boost domestic manufacturing across various sectors, including defence.
'Aatmanirbhar Bharat' (Self-Reliant India) calls for reducing import dependence and promoting indigenous production.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of indigenization in the defence sector for India's national security and economic development.
Discuss the various policies and initiatives undertaken by the government to promote 'Make in India' in defence, evaluating their effectiveness.
Examine the challenges faced by the domestic defence manufacturing industry and suggest measures to overcome them.
Evaluate the role of the Defence Acquisition Council in streamlining procurement processes and fostering a self-reliant defence ecosystem.
articles.imageDescription: Modern defence equipment, such as a missile system, being manufactured in an Indian factory.
articles.trivia: India is one of the world's largest importers of defence equipment, a trend the government is actively trying to reverse through indigenisation.

articles.headline: National Urban Digital Mission (NUDM) Launches Phase 2 to Accelerate Smart City Initiatives
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs announced the launch of Phase 2 of the National Urban Digital Mission (NUDM), aiming to further digitize urban governance and services.
• NUDM Phase 2 focuses on expanding the digital infrastructure to more cities, promoting data-driven urban planning, and enhancing citizen engagement through digital platforms.
• New modules include advanced urban planning tools, digital grievance redressal systems, and smart mobility solutions.
• The mission seeks to create a robust, secure, and inclusive urban digital ecosystem that caters to the diverse needs of urban dwellers.
• Public-private partnerships and capacity building for municipal bodies are key components of this expanded phase.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Development processes and the development industry the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
articles.source: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
prelimsPoints: National Urban Digital Mission (NUDM) aims to create a shared digital infrastructure for urban India.
It operates within the framework of the Smart Cities Mission and other urban development initiatives.
Focus areas include urban planning, service delivery, grievance redressal, and citizen engagement.
The mission promotes the adoption of open standards and interoperable digital platforms for urban services.
mainsPoints: Analyze the potential of digital technologies to transform urban governance and improve the quality of life in Indian cities, with reference to NUDM.
Discuss the challenges in implementing large-scale urban digital initiatives, including issues of digital divide, data security, and capacity building for municipal bodies.
Examine how NUDM can contribute to achieving the objectives of the Smart Cities Mission and fostering sustainable urban development.
Suggest strategies to enhance citizen participation and ensure inclusivity in the design and delivery of digital urban services.
articles.imageDescription: A futuristic cityscape with digital overlays, symbolizing smart urban development and technology integration.
articles.trivia: India's urban population is projected to reach 600 million by 2030, making digital urban solutions critical for sustainable growth.

articles.headline: RBI Hints at Broader Rollout of Digital Rupee (CBDC) Pilot, Focus on Interoperability
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) indicated plans to expand the pilot projects for the Digital Rupee (e-₹), India's Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC).
• The focus of the expansion will be on enhancing interoperability with existing payment systems and increasing public awareness and adoption.
• The RBI aims to address initial challenges observed in the wholesale and retail CBDC pilots, such as transaction speed and user experience.
• The Digital Rupee is envisioned to offer a secure, efficient, and innovative payment alternative, complementing physical cash and other digital payment methods.
• Discussions are ongoing with banks and payment service providers to integrate e-₹ seamlessly into the financial ecosystem.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC) is a digital form of a country's fiat currency, issued by the central bank.
The Digital Rupee (e-₹) is India's CBDC, launched by the RBI in pilot phases (wholesale in Nov 2022, retail in Dec 2022).
CBDCs can be based on Distributed Ledger Technology (DLT) like blockchain, or other centralized technologies.
It aims to reduce operational costs, promote financial inclusion, and provide a safer alternative to private cryptocurrencies.
mainsPoints: Analyze the potential benefits and risks associated with the introduction of a Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC) in India, focusing on financial stability and monetary policy.
Discuss how the Digital Rupee (e-₹) can transform India's payment landscape, enhance financial inclusion, and compete with private cryptocurrencies.
Examine the technological and regulatory challenges in implementing a nationwide CBDC, including issues of data privacy, cybersecurity, and interoperability.
Evaluate the global trends in CBDC development and India's position in the evolving landscape of digital finance.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized image of a digital rupee symbol on a smartphone screen, representing the Central Bank Digital Currency.
articles.trivia: Globally, over 100 countries are exploring or developing their own CBDCs, with some already launching pilots or full-scale implementations.

highlights: C. P. Radhakrishnan takes oath as the 15th Vice President of India, emphasizing the constitutional role and responsibilities.
Ten Naxalites killed in a major anti-insurgency operation by security forces in Chhattisgarh's Gariaband district.
Tragic truck accident during Ganesh Chaturthi celebrations in Karnataka's Hassan district claims 10 lives, raising road safety concerns.
India reviews progress on the ambitious India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor (IMEC), aiming to boost global connectivity.
ISRO successfully launches an advanced Earth Observation Satellite to enhance resource mapping and disaster management capabilities.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the Vice President of India, consider the following statements:
1. The Vice President is elected by an electoral college consisting of the elected members of both Houses of Parliament.
2. The Vice President acts as the ex-officio Chairman of the Rajya Sabha.
3. The Vice President can be removed from office by a resolution passed by a majority of all the then members of the Rajya Sabha and agreed to by the Lok Sabha.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: The electoral college for the Vice President consists of *all* members of both Houses of Parliament (elected and nominated), not just elected members. Statement 2 is correct: Article 64 of the Constitution states that the Vice President shall be ex-officio Chairman of the Council of States (Rajya Sabha). Statement 3 is correct: Article 67(b) outlines the procedure for the removal of the Vice President.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is the highest decision-making body in the Ministry of Defence for defence procurements in India?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Defence Acquisition Council (DAC) is the highest decision-making body in the Ministry of Defence concerning new policy and capital acquisitions for the three services (Army, Navy, Air Force) and the Indian Coast Guard. It is chaired by the Defence Minister.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Forest Rights Act (FRA), 2006:
1. It recognizes individual and community forest rights of Scheduled Tribes and Other Traditional Forest Dwellers.
2. The Gram Sabha has a crucial role in the process of recognition and vesting of forest rights.
3. The Act primarily focuses on commercial exploitation of forest resources by forest dwellers.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statements 1 and 2 are correct. The Forest Rights Act, 2006 (FRA), recognizes the rights of forest-dwelling communities and empowers Gram Sabhas in the process. Statement 3 is incorrect; the Act's primary focus is on recognizing and vesting forest rights, including usage and management, but not primarily on commercial exploitation in a way that undermines conservation.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following statements best describes the India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor (IMEC)?
options: (a) It is a military alliance aimed at countering maritime threats in the Indian Ocean.
(b) It is a cultural exchange program promoting tourism between India, the Middle East, and Europe.
(c) It is a proposed economic corridor leveraging sea and rail networks to enhance connectivity and trade between India, the Middle East, and Europe.
(d) It is a development assistance program focusing on infrastructure projects in least developed countries.
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) It is a proposed economic corridor leveraging sea and rail networks to enhance connectivity and trade between India, the Middle East, and Europe.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor (IMEC) was announced during the G20 Summit in 2023 with the objective of enhancing connectivity and economic integration through sea and rail links, thereby facilitating trade and cooperation.

prelimsMocks.question:
With respect to the Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC) in India, consider the following statements:
1. The Digital Rupee (e-₹) is issued by commercial banks and regulated by the Reserve Bank of India.
2. Both wholesale and retail pilot projects for the Digital Rupee have been launched.
3. CBDCs are designed to replace all forms of physical cash in the economy.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: The Digital Rupee (e-₹) is issued by the Reserve Bank of India, not commercial banks, making it sovereign currency. Commercial banks are intermediaries for distribution. Statement 2 is correct: RBI launched wholesale pilot in Nov 2022 and retail pilot in Dec 2022. Statement 3 is incorrect: CBDCs are intended to complement, not replace, physical cash and existing digital payment systems.

mainsMocks.question: Analyze the multi-faceted challenges in ensuring road safety in India, particularly during large public gatherings and festivals. Suggest comprehensive strategies involving technology, policy, and public awareness to mitigate such risks. (15 marks, 250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS1: Social issues; GS3: Disaster Management
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the context of road accidents and festival crowd management. Discuss challenges like driver behavior, infrastructure, enforcement, and crowd dynamics. Suggest solutions including stricter laws, smart traffic management, public awareness campaigns, better event planning, and emergency response improvements.

mainsMocks.question: Critically examine India's strategy to combat Left-Wing Extremism (LWE), focusing on the 'SAMADHAN' doctrine. Discuss the balance between security operations and development initiatives, and the challenges in achieving long-term peace in affected regions. (15 marks, 250 words)
mainsMocks.approach: Define LWE and its prevalence. Explain the SAMADHAN doctrine (Smart leadership, Aggressive strategy, Motivation and training, Actionable intelligence, Dashboard-based KPIs, Harnessing technology, Action plan for each theatre, No access to financing). Evaluate its effectiveness, highlighting successes in security ops and challenges in development, governance, and human rights. Conclude with a balanced approach for sustainable peace.

mainsMocks.question: Discuss the strategic and economic significance of the India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor (IMEC) for India in the context of global connectivity and trade. How does IMEC contribute to India's broader foreign policy objectives and its role in the Indo-Pacific region? (15 marks, 250 words)
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce IMEC's goals and components. Explain its economic benefits for India (trade routes, supply chain resilience, investment). Analyze its strategic importance (geopolitical influence, counter-balance to other initiatives, strengthening ties with partners). Connect IMEC to India's Act West policy, Indo-Pacific vision, and role as a global power. Conclude on its potential to reshape global trade and geopolitics.

quickBytes.text: The Vice President of India is the ex-officio Chairman of the Rajya Sabha, presiding over its sessions.
The 'Red Corridor' refers to regions in India affected by Left-Wing Extremism (Naxalism).
The Motor Vehicles (Amendment) Act, 2019, aims to improve road safety with stricter penalties.
IMEC (India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor) was announced during the G20 Summit in 2023.
PSLV (Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle) is ISRO's workhorse for launching Earth observation satellites.
The Forest Rights Act, 2006, empowers Gram Sabhas in recognizing tribal forest rights.
The RBI's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) sets the benchmark repo rate to manage inflation.
National Quantum Mission aims to position India as a leader in quantum technologies by 2031.
AgriStack is a digital public infrastructure initiative to modernize India's agriculture sector.
The Digital Rupee (e-₹) is India's Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC), issued by the RBI.

GS1.headline: Tragic Truck Accident Claims 10 Lives During Ganesh Chaturthi Celebrations in Karnataka's Hassan District
GS1.explainer:
• Ten people were killed and 22 others injured when a speeding truck ran over a crowd celebrating Ganesh Chaturthi in Hassan district, Karnataka.
• The incident highlights critical issues related to road safety, crowd management during festivals, and enforcement of traffic regulations.
• Local authorities have launched an investigation into the cause of the accident, including potential negligence by the driver and event organizers.
• The state government has announced ex-gratia payments for the victims and assured support for the injured.
• This tragedy underscores the need for stricter adherence to safety protocols and better infrastructure management, especially during large public gatherings.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of Indian Society, Diversity of India.
Social empowerment, communalism, regionalism & secularism.
Urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
prelimsPoints: Ganesh Chaturthi is a popular Hindu festival celebrating the birth of Lord Ganesha.
The Motor Vehicles (Amendment) Act, 2019, aims to improve road safety by imposing stricter penalties.
National Road Safety Policy focuses on education, engineering (roads and vehicles), enforcement, and emergency care.
Disaster Management Act, 2005, provides for effective management of disasters and matters connected therewith.
mainsPoints: Analyze the multi-faceted challenges in ensuring road safety in India, including driver behavior, infrastructure deficiencies, and enforcement gaps.
Discuss effective strategies for crowd management during religious festivals and public events to prevent such tragedies.
Evaluate the role of technology and public awareness campaigns in promoting safer road practices and reducing accident fatalities.
Examine the institutional framework for disaster response and victim compensation in India, suggesting areas for improvement.
GS1.imageDescription: Emergency services at the site of a truck accident in Hassan, Karnataka, with local residents gathered.
GS1.trivia: India accounts for about 11% of the total number of road accident deaths in the world, despite having only 1% of the world's vehicles.

GS1.headline: India Nominates Traditional Art Form for UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage List
GS1.explainer:
• India has formally nominated a unique traditional art form from one of its states for inscription on UNESCO's Representative List of the Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity.
• The nomination highlights the rich cultural diversity and historical significance of the art, which encompasses traditional performing arts, rituals, and craftsmanship.
• Extensive documentation and community participation were involved in preparing the nomination dossier, emphasizing the living traditions and their intergenerational transmission.
• If inscribed, it would bring global recognition to the art form, aiding in its preservation and promoting cultural tourism.
• This move underscores India's commitment to safeguarding its diverse cultural heritage and sharing it with the world.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
prelimsPoints: UNESCO's Intangible Cultural Heritage list aims to safeguard important intangible cultural heritage worldwide.
India has several elements inscribed on this list, including Yoga, Kumbh Mela, and Traditional brass and copper craft of utensil making among the Thatheras of Jandiala Guru.
The Ministry of Culture is the nodal agency for coordinating India's nominations to UNESCO's ICH list.
ICH includes oral traditions, performing arts, social practices, rituals, festive events, knowledge and practices concerning nature and the universe, and traditional craftsmanship.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of UNESCO's Intangible Cultural Heritage list in preserving and promoting India's diverse living traditions.
Analyze the challenges involved in safeguarding traditional art forms and cultural practices in the face of modernization and globalization.
Examine the socio-economic benefits of inscribing an art form on the UNESCO list, including its impact on local communities and cultural tourism.
Suggest strategies for effective documentation, transmission, and promotion of India's intangible cultural heritage.
GS1.imageDescription: Artists performing a vibrant traditional Indian art form, showcasing intricate costumes and expressions.
GS1.trivia: The Convention for the Safeguarding of the Intangible Cultural Heritage was adopted by UNESCO in 2003.

GS2.headline: C. P. Radhakrishnan Sworn In as India's 15th Vice President
GS2.explainer:
• C. P. Radhakrishnan took the oath of office as the 15th Vice President of India, administered by the President of India at Rashtrapati Bhavan.
• His election followed a comprehensive electoral process involving members of both Houses of Parliament.
• The Vice President serves as the ex-officio Chairman of the Rajya Sabha, presiding over its sessions and maintaining decorum.
• The office plays a crucial role in the constitutional setup, acting as a link between the Executive and the Legislature.
• He previously served as Governor and was a prominent political figure, bringing significant administrative and legislative experience to the role.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies.
prelimsPoints: The Vice President is the second-highest constitutional office in India.
Elected by an electoral college consisting of members of both Houses of Parliament (Article 66).
Administered oath by the President (Article 69).
Ex-officio Chairman of the Rajya Sabha (Article 64).
Acts as President in case of vacancy or absence of the President (Article 65).
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the Vice President's role in India's parliamentary democracy, particularly as the Chairman of the Rajya Sabha.
Analyze the constitutional powers and functions of the Vice President and their impact on legislative proceedings.
Evaluate the electoral process for the Vice President and its democratic implications.
Examine the checks and balances provided by the office of the Vice President in the Indian constitutional framework.
GS2.imageDescription: C. P. Radhakrishnan taking oath as the 15th Vice President of India, with the President of India present.
GS2.trivia: India's first Vice President, Dr. Sarvepalli Radhakrishnan, later became the second President of India.

GS2.headline: Former Kerala Assembly Speaker P. P. Thankachan Passes Away at 86
GS2.explainer:
• P. P. Thankachan, a veteran politician and former Speaker of the Kerala Legislative Assembly, passed away at the age of 86.
• He served as a member of the Kerala Legislative Assembly from 1982 to 2001 and held the Speaker's position from 1991 to 1995.
• Thankachan was known for his significant contributions to state politics and his role in upholding parliamentary traditions.
• His career spanned several decades, witnessing crucial legislative developments and political shifts in Kerala.
• The demise marks the end of an era for a prominent voice in Kerala's political landscape.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary Ministries and Departments of the Government; pressure groups and formal/informal associations and their role in the Polity.
prelimsPoints: The Speaker of a State Legislative Assembly is the presiding officer of the Assembly.
Elected by the members of the Assembly from amongst themselves.
Maintains order and decorum, interprets rules, and certifies money bills.
Can be removed by a resolution passed by a majority of all the then members of the Assembly.
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical role of the Speaker in ensuring the smooth functioning and integrity of a State Legislative Assembly.
Analyze how the Speaker's position contributes to maintaining parliamentary democracy at the state level.
Examine the challenges faced by Speakers in upholding impartiality amidst political pressures.
GS2.imageDescription: A portrait of P. P. Thankachan, former Speaker of the Kerala Legislative Assembly.
GS2.trivia: The first woman Speaker of any State Legislative Assembly in India was Shanno Devi (Haryana, 1966).

GS2.headline: India Reviews Progress on India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor (IMEC) with Key Partners
GS2.explainer:
• India held a high-level review meeting with partner nations to assess the progress and future roadmap of the India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor (IMEC).
• IMEC, announced during the G20 Summit in 2023, aims to enhance connectivity and economic integration across Asia, the Middle East, and Europe through sea and rail links.
• Discussions focused on accelerating infrastructure development, streamlining customs procedures, and ensuring financial viability of the ambitious project.
• The corridor is envisioned as a counter-narrative to other major connectivity initiatives, promoting stable and resilient supply chains.
• This initiative holds significant geopolitical and geoeconomic importance for India, enhancing its strategic outreach and trade routes.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: IMEC was launched during the G20 Summit in New Delhi in September 2023.
It comprises an Eastern Corridor (India to Arabian Gulf) and a Northern Corridor (Arabian Gulf to Europe).
Key partners include India, USA, Saudi Arabia, UAE, France, Germany, Italy, and the European Union.
Aims to include a railway network, ship-to-rail transit networks, and road transport routes.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic and economic significance of the IMEC for India in the context of global connectivity and trade.
Compare and contrast IMEC with other major connectivity initiatives, discussing its potential advantages and challenges.
Discuss how IMEC can foster greater regional cooperation and stability in the Middle East and Europe.
Examine the potential geopolitical implications of IMEC on India's foreign policy and its role in the Indo-Pacific.
GS2.imageDescription: A map illustrating the proposed routes of the India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor.
GS2.trivia: The IMEC project is seen as a major outcome of India's G20 presidency in 2023.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Reinforces Tribal Rights, Upholds Amendments to Forest Rights Act
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark judgment, upheld recent amendments to the Forest Rights Act (FRA), further strengthening the rights of tribal communities and forest dwellers.
• The ruling clarifies the process for recognizing community forest resource (CFR) rights and individual forest rights (IFRs), emphasizing Gram Sabha's pivotal role.
• Environmental groups and tribal organizations had challenged certain aspects, but the court affirmed the amendments' intent to balance conservation with tribal welfare.
• This decision is expected to have significant implications for forest governance, conservation efforts, and the empowerment of indigenous communities across the country.
• The judgment reiterated the 'prior informed consent' principle for any developmental projects in tribal areas.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: The Scheduled Tribes and Other Traditional Forest Dwellers (Recognition of Forest Rights) Act, 2006 (FRA), aims to recognize and vest forest rights and occupation in forest land to forest dwelling Scheduled Tribes and Other Traditional Forest Dwellers.
Gram Sabha plays a crucial role in the process of recognition and vesting of forest rights.
The Act recognizes four types of rights: Title rights, Use rights, Relief and development rights, and Forest management rights.
Community Forest Resource (CFR) rights empower Gram Sabhas to protect, regenerate, conserve, or manage any forest resources.
mainsPoints: Analyze the impact of the Forest Rights Act, 2006, on tribal empowerment and forest conservation in India, critically evaluating its implementation.
Discuss the role of the judiciary in balancing environmental protection with the rights of indigenous communities, with reference to recent judgments.
Examine the challenges in securing land and resource rights for tribal populations and suggest policy reforms for their effective implementation.
Evaluate the concept of 'prior informed consent' in the context of developmental projects in tribal areas and its implications for sustainable development.
GS2.imageDescription: A group of tribal community members gathered in a forest, symbolizing their connection to forest resources.
GS2.trivia: India has over 104 million tribal people, accounting for 8.6% of the country's population, as per the 2011 Census.

GS2.headline: India and France Hold Strategic Dialogue, Deepen Defence and Space Cooperation
GS2.explainer:
• India and France held their annual Strategic Dialogue, focusing on enhancing cooperation in defence, space, and emerging technologies.
• The dialogue reaffirmed the strong strategic partnership between the two nations, particularly in the Indo-Pacific region and counter-terrorism efforts.
• Discussions included joint defence manufacturing, technology transfer, and intelligence sharing, building on existing defence procurements.
• Both sides emphasized the importance of a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, aligning their visions for regional stability.
• The leaders also explored new avenues for collaboration in areas like artificial intelligence, cybersecurity, and green energy, reinforcing their comprehensive strategic partnership.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India and France established a strategic partnership in 1998.
France is a key partner for India in defence, space, and civil nuclear energy.
Joint military exercises include Varuna (Navy), Garuda (Air Force), and Shakti (Army).
Both countries are committed to multilateralism and a rules-based international order.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolution and significance of the India-France strategic partnership in the changing geopolitical landscape.
Discuss the key pillars of cooperation between India and France, particularly in defence, space, and clean energy.
Examine how the India-France partnership contributes to stability and security in the Indo-Pacific region.
Evaluate the potential for further collaboration in emerging technologies and its implications for India's technological self-reliance.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and France displayed together, symbolizing bilateral cooperation.
GS2.trivia: France was one of the first countries to establish a strategic dialogue with India, reflecting the depth of their relationship.

GS2.headline: India Advocates for Global South at UN General Assembly, Calls for Multilateral Reforms
GS2.explainer:
• India's External Affairs Minister addressed the UN General Assembly (UNGA), emphasizing the need for greater representation and voice for the Global South in global governance.
• The speech highlighted India's commitment to multilateralism but stressed the urgency of reforming international institutions, including the UN Security Council.
• Key issues raised included climate justice, equitable access to resources, sustainable development goals (SDGs), and addressing global inequalities.
• India reiterated its stance on counter-terrorism and called for a comprehensive convention to tackle the menace effectively.
• The address underscored India's growing leadership role as a voice for developing nations and its vision for a more inclusive world order.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: The UN General Assembly (UNGA) is the main deliberative, policymaking, and representative organ of the United Nations.
The concept of 'Global South' refers to countries in Asia, Africa, and Latin America, often characterized by developing economies and shared historical experiences.
India has consistently advocated for reforms of the UN Security Council, seeking permanent membership.
Climate Justice emphasizes that those who have contributed least to climate change suffer the most from its impacts and should receive support.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's increasing role as a voice for the Global South and its implications for global governance and international relations.
Discuss the necessity and challenges of reforming multilateral institutions like the United Nations, particularly the Security Council, to reflect contemporary realities.
Examine India's stance on key global issues such as climate change, sustainable development, and counter-terrorism at international forums.
Evaluate the effectiveness of India's diplomatic efforts in advocating for the interests of developing nations on the world stage.
GS2.imageDescription: The United Nations General Assembly hall during a session, with country flags visible.
GS2.trivia: The first session of the UN General Assembly was convened on 10 January 1946 in London.

GS2.headline: National Urban Digital Mission (NUDM) Launches Phase 2 to Accelerate Smart City Initiatives
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs announced the launch of Phase 2 of the National Urban Digital Mission (NUDM), aiming to further digitize urban governance and services.
• NUDM Phase 2 focuses on expanding the digital infrastructure to more cities, promoting data-driven urban planning, and enhancing citizen engagement through digital platforms.
• New modules include advanced urban planning tools, digital grievance redressal systems, and smart mobility solutions.
• The mission seeks to create a robust, secure, and inclusive urban digital ecosystem that caters to the diverse needs of urban dwellers.
• Public-private partnerships and capacity building for municipal bodies are key components of this expanded phase.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Development processes and the development industry the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
GS2.source: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
prelimsPoints: National Urban Digital Mission (NUDM) aims to create a shared digital infrastructure for urban India.
It operates within the framework of the Smart Cities Mission and other urban development initiatives.
Focus areas include urban planning, service delivery, grievance redressal, and citizen engagement.
The mission promotes the adoption of open standards and interoperable digital platforms for urban services.
mainsPoints: Analyze the potential of digital technologies to transform urban governance and improve the quality of life in Indian cities, with reference to NUDM.
Discuss the challenges in implementing large-scale urban digital initiatives, including issues of digital divide, data security, and capacity building for municipal bodies.
Examine how NUDM can contribute to achieving the objectives of the Smart Cities Mission and fostering sustainable urban development.
Suggest strategies to enhance citizen participation and ensure inclusivity in the design and delivery of digital urban services.
GS2.imageDescription: A futuristic cityscape with digital overlays, symbolizing smart urban development and technology integration.
GS2.trivia: India's urban population is projected to reach 600 million by 2030, making digital urban solutions critical for sustainable growth.

GS3.headline: Ten Naxalites Killed in Major Clash with Security Forces in Chhattisgarh's Gariaband
GS3.explainer:
• A significant anti-Naxal operation in Chhattisgarh's Gariaband district resulted in the killing of ten Naxalites during a fierce encounter with security forces.
• The operation was a joint effort by district reserve guard (DRG) and special task force (STF) personnel, targeting a known Naxal hideout.
• This incident underscores the ongoing challenge of Left-Wing Extremism (LWE) in several parts of India, particularly in the 'Red Corridor' states.
• Security forces recovered a cache of arms, ammunition, and other Naxal literature from the encounter site.
• The government continues its dual strategy of robust security operations combined with development initiatives to combat LWE.
gsSubTopics: Linkages between development and spread of extremism.
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security.

Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention.
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
prelimsPoints: Gariaband district is located in Chhattisgarh, a state significantly affected by LWE.
Security forces involved in anti-Naxal operations include CRPF, BSF, ITBP, and state police forces like DRG and STF.
The 'Red Corridor' refers to regions in central, eastern, and southern India affected by Naxalite-Maoist insurgency.

Key strategies against LWE include 'SAMADHAN' (Smart leadership, Aggressive strategy, Motivation and training, Actionable intelligence, Dashboard-based KPIs, Harnessing technology, Action plan for each theatre, No access to financing).
mainsPoints: Analyze the root causes and socio-economic factors contributing to the spread of Left-Wing Extremism in India.
Discuss the effectiveness of India's multi-pronged strategy (security and development) in tackling LWE, citing recent successes and challenges.
Examine the human rights concerns and ethical dilemmas faced by security forces in counter-insurgency operations.
Suggest policy measures for sustainable peace and development in Naxal-affected regions, focusing on infrastructure, governance, and tribal rights.
GS3.imageDescription: Security forces patrolling a forested area in Chhattisgarh during an anti-Naxal operation.
GS3.trivia: The term 'Naxalite' originated from the Naxalbari village in West Bengal, where a peasant uprising took place in 1967.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Earth Observation Satellite for Enhanced Resource Mapping
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched an advanced Earth Observation Satellite (EOS) from Sriharikota, marking another milestone in India's space program.
• The satellite, carried by a PSLV rocket, is equipped with high-resolution imaging sensors for detailed mapping of natural resources, agriculture, and urban development.
• Its data will be crucial for disaster management, environmental monitoring, and climate change studies, providing timely and accurate information.
• This mission reinforces India's indigenous capabilities in satellite technology and its commitment to using space for national development.
• The launch further strengthens India's constellation of remote sensing satellites, offering comprehensive coverage and data services.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: ISRO is India's national space agency.
PSLV (Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle) is ISRO's third-generation launch vehicle, primarily used for launching Earth observation and remote sensing satellites.
Earth observation satellites provide data for applications like agriculture, forestry, urban planning, disaster warning, and oceanography.
Sriharikota in Andhra Pradesh is India's primary spaceport, home to the Satish Dhawan Space Centre (SDSC).
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of Earth Observation Satellites in addressing India's developmental challenges, including food security, water management, and disaster mitigation.
Analyze India's achievements in space technology and its contributions to global space exploration and applications.
Examine the socio-economic benefits derived from space-based applications in various sectors of the Indian economy.
Discuss the challenges and future prospects of India's space program, including the increasing role of private sector participation.
GS3.imageDescription: A PSLV rocket lifting off from the launch pad at Sriharikota, carrying an Earth Observation Satellite.
GS3.trivia: India is one of the few countries with advanced capabilities in building and launching remote sensing satellites, with its first such satellite, Bhaskara-I, launched in 1979.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate, Focuses on Inflation Control Amid Global Uncertainties
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its latest meeting, deciding to maintain the repo rate at its current level.
• The decision reflects a cautious approach, balancing inflation control with the need to support economic growth amidst global economic uncertainties and domestic inflationary pressures.
• RBI Governor highlighted the global headwinds, including geopolitical tensions and commodity price volatility, as key factors influencing the policy stance.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to aligning inflation with the target while supporting growth, indicating a 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance.
• Experts anticipate continued vigilance from the central bank, with future policy moves dependent on evolving economic data and inflation trajectory.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.

Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.

Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
prelimsPoints: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a six-member body responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) in India.
The primary objective of monetary policy is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
Repo rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks, impacting the cost of borrowing for individuals and businesses.
Inflation targeting framework in India aims to keep Consumer Price Index (CPI) inflation at 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
mainsPoints: Analyze the current challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control and economic growth, considering domestic and global factors.
Discuss the effectiveness of the Monetary Policy Committee's decisions in achieving its mandated objectives of price stability and growth.
Examine the implications of a 'status quo' policy stance on various sectors of the Indian economy, including lending, investment, and consumption.
Evaluate the role of fiscal policy in complementing monetary policy to achieve macroeconomic stability and inclusive growth.
